title: MLOps & GenAIOps Engineer Associate (AI-300)
subtitle: Operationalise Machine Learning and Generative AI Solutions
description: Learn to operationalise machine learning and generative AI solutions with MLOps pipelines, Azure Machine Learning, MLflow, Microsoft Foundry, and CI/CD automation
overview: This advanced course prepares you for the AI-300 certification exam by covering the full MLOps and GenAIOps lifecycle. You will learn to design MLOps infrastructure, manage ML model lifecycles with MLflow and Azure Machine Learning pipelines, build GenAIOps infrastructure with Microsoft Foundry, implement quality assurance and observability for generative AI, and optimise GenAI systems through RAG tuning and fine-tuning. The course follows the official exam study guide weighting and combines infrastructure automation with hands-on model operations.

learningObjectives.title: MLOps Infrastructure Design
learningObjectives.description: Design and provision MLOps infrastructure with Azure Machine Learning workspaces, compute targets, and infrastructure as code using Bicep

learningObjectives.title: ML Model Lifecycle & Operations
learningObjectives.description: Manage the complete ML model lifecycle with MLflow tracking, Azure Machine Learning pipelines, model registry, and automated retraining

learningObjectives.title: GenAIOps Infrastructure
learningObjectives.description: Build and manage GenAIOps infrastructure with Microsoft Foundry, prompt flow orchestration, and deployment pipelines for generative AI

learningObjectives.title: GenAI Quality Assurance & Observability
learningObjectives.description: Implement quality assurance frameworks and observability for generative AI solutions, including evaluation metrics, content safety, and drift detection

learningObjectives.title: Optimising Generative AI Systems
learningObjectives.description: Optimise generative AI systems through RAG pipeline tuning, fine-tuning strategies, and prompt optimisation for accuracy and cost

modules.title: MLOps Infrastructure Design (15-20%)
modules.description: Design and provision the infrastructure foundation for MLOps workflows
topics: Azure Machine Learning workspace architecture and configuration
Compute targets: compute instances, clusters, and serverless compute
Infrastructure as Code with Bicep for ML environments
Networking and security for ML workspaces: private endpoints and managed VNets
Data stores and data asset management
Role-based access control for ML resources
Environment management: curated and custom environments
Cost management strategies for ML compute

modules.title: ML Model Lifecycle & Operations (25-30%)
modules.description: Manage the end-to-end ML model lifecycle from experiment tracking to production deployment
topics: MLflow experiment tracking: metrics, parameters, and artefacts
Azure Machine Learning pipelines: components, data flow, and scheduling
Model registry: versioning, staging, and promotion workflows
Automated retraining with pipeline triggers and data drift detection
Model packaging and deployment to managed online endpoints
Batch inference pipelines for large-scale scoring
A/B testing and traffic splitting for model deployments
GitHub Actions CI/CD pipelines for ML model delivery
Feature engineering and feature store integration
Responsible AI dashboards for model fairness and interpretability

modules.title: GenAIOps Infrastructure (20-25%)
modules.description: Build and manage infrastructure for generative AI operations with Microsoft Foundry
topics: Microsoft Foundry setup and project organisation
Prompt flow orchestration: flows, tools, and connections
Deployment pipelines for generative AI solutions
Model catalogue management and deployment configuration
Connection management for AI services and data sources
Infrastructure automation for GenAIOps environments
Version control and collaboration for prompt flows
Integration with Azure DevOps and GitHub for GenAI CI/CD

modules.title: GenAI Quality Assurance & Observability (10-15%)
modules.description: Implement quality frameworks and monitoring for generative AI in production
topics: Evaluation metrics for generative AI: groundedness, relevance, coherence
Automated evaluation pipelines with prompt flow evaluators
Content safety configuration and monitoring
Production observability: logging, tracing, and alerting for GenAI
Drift detection for generative AI outputs
Human-in-the-loop review workflows
Red-teaming and adversarial testing frameworks

modules.title: Optimising Generative AI Systems (10-15%)
modules.description: Optimise RAG pipelines, implement fine-tuning, and improve prompt effectiveness
topics: RAG pipeline optimisation: chunking strategies, re-ranking, and hybrid search
Fine-tuning foundation models on Azure: data preparation and training
Prompt optimisation techniques for accuracy and token efficiency
Model selection and benchmarking for specific use cases
Caching strategies for inference cost reduction
Performance profiling and bottleneck identification
Scaling inference endpoints for variable demand

handsOnLabs.title: MLflow Tracking & Azure ML Pipeline
handsOnLabs.description: Set up MLflow experiment tracking and build an Azure Machine Learning pipeline with automated data processing and model training

handsOnLabs.title: Model Registry & Deployment Pipeline
handsOnLabs.description: Register a trained model, create a CI/CD pipeline with GitHub Actions, and deploy to a managed online endpoint with traffic splitting

handsOnLabs.title: GenAIOps with Prompt Flow
handsOnLabs.description: Build a prompt flow orchestration, configure evaluation metrics, and deploy with automated quality gates

handsOnLabs.title: RAG Pipeline Optimisation
handsOnLabs.description: Optimise a RAG pipeline by experimenting with chunking strategies, re-ranking models, and evaluation metrics

prerequisites: Experience with Azure Machine Learning SDK or studio
Basic DevOps concepts: CI/CD, version control, infrastructure as code
Data science background: model training, evaluation, and feature engineering
targetAudience: ML engineers operationalising models for production
Data scientists transitioning to MLOps practices
DevOps engineers supporting ML and GenAI workloads
AI engineers building automated ML pipelines

instructor.name: Yair Knijn
instructor.title: Principal Cloud Architect & Engineer
instructor.bio: Microsoft Certified Trainer with hands-on experience operationalising machine learning and generative AI solutions on Azure. Specialises in building automated ML pipelines, MLflow-based model management, and GenAIOps workflows for enterprise organisations.
certifications: Microsoft Certified: Azure AI Engineer Associate
Microsoft Certified: Azure DevOps Engineer Expert

certification.name: Microsoft Certified: Operationalizing Machine Learning and Generative AI Solutions

personalStory.story: The gap between a working notebook and a reliable production model is where most ML projects fail. I have spent years helping teams cross that gap -- setting up pipelines that retrain automatically when data shifts, building deployment workflows that do not require a data scientist to SSH into a VM at 2 AM, and now extending those same principles to generative AI with GenAIOps. This course is the playbook I wish existed when I started operationalising my first models.
